title: Build a structured-output pipeline
description: Construct the schema, parse, validate, and bounded-repair stages so a call reliably yields conforming structure or a typed failure.
body: Steps: 1. Define the strict output schema (required fields, types, enums). 2. Request the structure (response_format / function schema where the provider supports it). 3. Parse and validate every response. 4. On failure, feed the validator error back for at most N repairs. 5. After the budget, return a typed error and do not act. Rollback point: before relaxing the schema to make parsing pass. Validation: a conformance run over sampled inputs reports the pass rate and malformed cases reach the typed fallback rather than downstream code.
